contents.markdown:
# Video: 00:00.733 => 00:43.233
Width: 1080
Height: 608

Transcript
```
WEBVTT

00:01.360 --> 00:06.640
<Speaker 1>When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

00:07.120 --> 00:13.320
<Speaker 2>To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

00:13.440 --> 00:23.680
<Speaker 1>We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

00:24.040 --> 00:29.120
<Speaker 3>What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

00:29.600 --> 00:32.880
<Speaker 3>And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

00:33.680 --> 00:37.200
<Speaker 4>Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.

00:38.680 --> 00:41.280
<Speaker 4>9555 request to end pushback received.
```

Summary.valueString: The video opens with a scenic aerial view of an island and a small plane flying, introducing a collaboration between Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI. It then transitions to a discussion about neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology, emphasizing the importance of high-quality data. Visuals include waveform audio data and shots of data centers and server farms, highlighting the scale of data used to build a universal TTS model with 3,000 hours of audio. The narration explains how this extensive data enables the model to capture audio nuances and produce natural-sounding voices. The segment concludes with a demonstration of the high fidelity of cognitive services' TTS, followed by simulated airport ground control communications and visuals of airplanes taxiing and preparing for pushback at an airport, ending with the Microsoft logo.
